title: English Chativa · Practice Item Schema (Grade 7)
description: Schema of one practice item. Maps to Curriculum 2020 master (curriculum-2020-master.md) across 3 layers: content (can-do + vocab + grammar + domain), measurement (LSRW + EPA), and organization (unit + track + envelope).

content_layer.description: Curriculum 2020 master tags (the WHAT — pedagogical content)

can_do_id.description: CEFR can-do statement id. References can-do-statements-grade7.json. Example: 'self-introduce-3-sentences'.

vocab_ids.description: Vocabulary items used. References vocab-band-1-grade7.json. Empty array if grammar-only or phonics-only.

grammar_id.description: Grammar structure exercised. References grammar-structures-grade7.json. Null if vocab-only or listening comprehension.

domain.description: One of 5 Curriculum 2020 domains. Reported to teacher/inspector — NOT shown to student.

measurement_layer.description: What BKT and EPA will track when this item runs

lsrw_primary.description: Primary BKT axis updated. One of 5. 'Language' = grammar/vocab integrated.

lsrw_secondary.description: Additional BKT axes touched (e.g. Listen-then-Speak). May be empty.

tnufa_measured.description: True if this skill is measured by official Tnufa Grade 9 (Reading, Writing, Language). False for Listening + Speaking (enrichment only).

expected_failure_axes.description: EPA tagging — what failure pattern is THIS item likely to expose. Used by intervention-matcher post-hoc, NOT during selection.

organization_layer.description: How this item is filed, scheduled, and presented

unit_id.description: Which Tnufa Unit this item belongs to. Aligned with curriculum/tnufa-plans/english-tnufa-grade7.json.

track.description: 1=Basic(red·Pre-A1) · 2=Intermediate(orange·A1-) · 3=Advanced(yellow·A1) · 4=Extension(green·A1+). Track 4 may include scaffolding from earlier units.

cefr_level.description: CEFR sub-level. Determines item difficulty parameters.

envelope.description: Game mechanic / interaction type. Maps to one of the existing JS mechanic templates in underwater-app/js/templates/.

content.description: The actual content rendered to the student. Schema varies by envelope.

examples.prompt_text: Sarah moved to London last month. She _____ from Tel Aviv.

narrative_context.description: Optional story-arc threading. Allows items to reference the year-long character journey (e.g. Sarah in London → Mumbai). NOT required for all items.

approved_by.description: מי אישר פדגוגית. Empty until Meytal or PA reviewer signs off.

source.description: Where the content came from. Examples: 'tnufa-grade7-week-6', 'CEFR-A1-can-do-1', 'original'.

_design_notes: 3-layer separation enforced: content / measurement / organization in named sub-objects.
BKT will update ONLY measurement_layer.lsrw_primary (and lsrw_secondary if present). NO sub-BKT per can-do/vocab/grammar — that would cause Q-matrix collapse.
EPA reads expected_failure_axes only AFTER an attempt — it's tagging, not selection criteria.
Track determines difficulty, NOT student level. A student can be on Track 4 of Unit 3 while still on Track 2 of Unit 4 — independent per Unit.
narrative_context is optional. MVP can ship without it. Story arc is enrichment, not mechanical.
Inherits from avnei-yesod item schema (architecture-mvp.md §6.3) — same separation principles, different axis names.
